seoTitle: Falmouth vs Fern Tree — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Falmouth and Fern Tree. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Falmouth vs Fern Tree: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Falmouth and Fern Tree.
heading: Falmouth vs Fern Tree — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Falmouth, Tasmania and Fern Tree, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Fern Tree has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Falmouth (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Falmouth or Fern Tree safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Falmouth has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Fern Tree scores 20/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Falmouth scores 26/100 for bushfire risk. Fern Tree scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Falmouth or Fern Tree?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Fern Tree has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.22% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
